2016-05-26.title: U.S. Urges No Bail for Reza Zarrab in Iran Sanctions Case
2016-05-26.abstract: Federal prosecutors said Mr. Zarrab, a prominent Turkish-based gold trader who is jailed in New York, was a flight risk.

2016-05-26.title: Azerbaijan Frees Khadija Ismayilova, Journalist Who Exposed Official Corruption
2016-05-26.abstract: The release of Ms. Ismayilova was welcomed by international rights groups after her incarceration had become an embarrassment to Azerbaijan’s president.

2016-05-26.title: Q. and A.: Ben Hillman and Gray Tuttle on Ethnic Unrest in Xinjiang and Tibet
2016-05-26.abstract: In an interview, Mr. Hillman and Mr. Tuttle discussed the grievances behind recent protests and the likelihood of their resolution.

2016-05-26.title: Korean Words, Straight From the Elephant’s Mouth
2016-05-26.abstract: While parrots are supposed to, well, parrot human speech, elephants aren’t. But don’t tell that to Koshik, an Asian elephant at a zoo near Seoul who can say at least five words.

2016-05-27.title: Reduction of Nuclear Arsenal Has Slowed Under Obama, Report Finds
2016-05-27.abstract: As President Obama prepares to visit Hiroshima, Japan, a new census from the Pentagon details the administration’s efforts to dismantle warheads.

2016-05-27.title: Press Freedom Group’s Application for U.N. Accreditation is Rejected
2016-05-27.abstract: The Committee to Protect Journalists had sought accreditation to freely roam the halls of the United Nations, but a panel including Azerbaijan, China and Pakistan rejected it.

2016-05-27.title: Times Readers’ Advice for Obama in Hiroshima: Focus on Nuclear-Free Future
2016-05-27.abstract: Some debated whether the president should apologize for the U.S. dropping the bomb on Japan. Others urged him to focus on a tomorrow without such weapons.

2016-05-27.title: Survivors Recount Horrors of Hiroshima and Nagasaki
2016-05-27.abstract: More than 120,000 people who lived through the atomic bombings in 1945 are still alive. Several survivors shared their stories, and thoughts about the president’s visit.

2016-05-27.title: At Hiroshima Memorial, Obama Says Nuclear Arms Require ‘Moral Revolution’
2016-05-27.abstract: “Technological progress without an equivalent progress in human institutions can doom us,” said Mr. Obama, the first sitting American president to visit the city.

2016-05-28.title: Desperate Times in Venezuela
2016-05-28.abstract: In response to economic crisis, the government has sharply curtailed official business, shut down schools and even reduced water and electricity supplies.

2016-05-28.title: Venezuela Drifts Into New Territory: Hunger, Blackouts and Government Shutdown
2016-05-28.abstract: The country is adjusting to a new normal of closed government and business offices, food shortages and cuts in water, electricity and phone service.

2016-05-28.title: Jane Fawcett, British Decoder Who Helped Doom the Bismarck, Dies at 95
2016-05-28.abstract: As an eagle-eyed staffer at Bletchley Park, home to Britain’s wartime code-breakers, Ms. Fawcett identified a message that led to the sinking of the fearsome German warship.

2016-05-28.title: China Promoting Tourism for Disputed Paracel Islands
2016-05-28.abstract: Some tropical islands in the South China Sea, where China is building military installations, are being advertised as sites for weddings or diving.

2016-05-28.title: Grass-Roots Anticorruption Drive Puts Heat on Mexican Lawmakers
2016-05-28.abstract: A public outcry has compelled the national legislature to debate a package of measures, which were the result of a citizens’ initiative, in a special session.

2016-05-29.title: Drought and ‘Rice First’ Policy Imperil Vietnamese Farmers
2016-05-29.abstract: The Mekong Delta, Vietnam’s premier rice growing region, is suffering its worst drought since at least 1926, and saline water has swept farther up the delta than ever.

2016-05-29.title: Eagle Catches Drone
2016-05-29.abstract: A handler demonstrates a trained eagle's ability to catch a drone in midflight.

2016-05-29.title: Dutch Firm Trains Eagles to Take Down High-Tech Prey: Drones
2016-05-29.abstract: When small, off-the-shelf models pose security or other threats, birds have the advantage of grounding them without a potentially dangerous crash.

2016-05-29.title: First Arrest Made in Gang Rape Case in Brazil
2016-05-29.abstract: A 16-year-old girl told the authorities that she had been attacked by more than 30 men, prompting outrage as the government vowed to combat crimes against women.

2016-05-29.title: Panama Papers May Inspire More Big Leaks, if Not Reform
2016-05-29.abstract: Action has been taken around the world in response to the disclosures, but the lucrative corporate registration business is likely to resist major change.

2016-05-30.title: Q. and A.: David Shambaugh on ‘China’s Future’
2016-05-30.abstract: The scholar discusses the country’s weaknesses, its parallels to other authoritarian states and the disenchantment felt by many American China-watchers.

2016-05-30.title: Hissène Habré, Ex-President of Chad, Is Convicted of War Crimes
2016-05-30.abstract: Mr. Habré, who led the African country from 1982 to 1990, was sentenced to life in prison for overseeing the suffering and death of his political enemies.

2016-05-30.title: Chinese Worker Detained for Photos of Liquor Labels Marking Tiananmen Crackdown
2016-05-30.abstract: A 29-year-old man was arrested in Chengdu on suspicion of “inciting subversion of state power” before the anniversary of the June 4, 1989, clampdown.

2016-05-30.title: On Offer in China: ‘Fried Swarm’ and Other Tasty Translations
2016-05-30.abstract: A stroll through just about any Chinese city will reveal the joys of eating in a country with language significantly far from English.

2016-05-30.title: Effort to Expose Russia’s ‘Troll Army’ Draws Vicious Retaliation
2016-05-30.abstract: Finland is an active front in an online “information war” between Russia and the West. When a journalist there confronted pro-Putin agitators, she became a target.

2016-05-31.title: For a Russian Couple, Safe Haven in Guatemala Is Fleeting
2016-05-31.abstract: Two entrepreneurs say that politics was behind the loss of their company at home, but they are accused of using fake documents in Guatemala and could be deported.

2016-05-31.title: Uganda Halts Military Cooperation With North Korea
2016-05-31.abstract: An announcement by Uganda on Monday amounts to a further isolation of North Korea in response to United Nations sanctions on its nuclear and missile activities.

2016-05-31.title: Brazil Gang Rape Victim Speaks Out
2016-05-31.abstract: The 16-year-old victim of a gang rape in Brazil has spoken to the media for the first time since the incident, giving details about her captors and the circumstances leading to the crime.

2016-05-31.title: Inmates in Syrian Prison Fear Reprisal After Revolt
2016-05-31.abstract: Tensions at a prison in Hama were so high on Monday night that prisoners feared that government forces would try to storm the compound by morning.

2016-05-31.title: Recording Spurs Anticorruption Minister to Resign in Brazil
2016-05-31.abstract: A secretly recorded conversation seemed to show that he tried to stymie the graft investigation involving the national oil company.
